_meta.description: Steel AeroBlade Double Deflection grille, long front blades, 3/4in blade spacing, with opposed blade damper (per GRD schedule note 1) -- used here as a RETURN grille
_meta.data_source_caveat: This cutsheet is confirmed SUPPLY-ONLY performance data (no return/exhaust content anywhere in its 7 pages; Titus's own product page describes 272RL itself as a Supply Grille). No return-specific 272RL performance table could be found. User's decision 2026-07-24: reuse the supply data as the reference for RG-2 anyway, reasoning actual return velocity through the same grille runs lower than the supply-rated bands regardless -- confirmed quantitatively: every one of the 32 breakpoints below corresponds to a core velocity of 600 FPM or lower (20 of 32 at 400 FPM, 6 at 500 FPM, 6 at 600 FPM), none reach 700+ FPM.
_meta.nc_correction_applied: +7 NC added to every raw table value, per cutsheet note: 'For deflection settings of 22.5 or 45, increase the stated sound levels by 1 or 7 NC respectively' (base table is referenced to 0 deg deflection)
_meta.model_correction_applied: 272 with damper = +0 NC, 1.00x pressure, 1.00x throw (no further adjustment needed) per Correction Factors for AeroBlade Supply Grilles table, page G22
_meta.target_nc: NC < 20 (raw table NC must be < 13 to survive the +7 correction) -- SAME as SD-4/SD-5 supply, as of this file. User has since asked (2026-07-24) to tighten RG-2 specifically to NC < 15 to force return sizing larger than supply for the same CFM, matching conventional lower-return-face-velocity design practice -- NOT YET BUILT, this file still reflects the interim NC<20 state shared with supply.
_meta.size_ceiling_decision: Capped at the '24x24'/'48x12/36x16' nominal size tier (core area 3.75-4.00 sq ft, max 1500 cfm), matching SD-4/SD-5 -- user has a separate pending request to raise this ceiling, not yet actioned (no target number given yet).
_meta.architecture: Identical Regular/Thin/Custom system as the supply sidewall family: Auto Width/Auto Height (instance, Length) carry this breakpoint cascade; Duct Width/Duct Height (instance, Length, tied to actual geometry) fall back through Custom Width/Custom Height (if Custom Size type flag is set and a nonzero override given) then Auto Width/Auto Height. Family renamed to 'RJA - Return Grate - Sidewall (CFM Auto-Sizing)' at some point this session (confirmed via document.Title, not renamed by this assistant).
_meta.removed_types_reason: Pre-rebuild family had these 3 Types (of its original 9) that did not correspond to any Titus 272RL published nominal size -- confirmed absent via full-text search of the cutsheet PDF. Removed 2026-07-24 during the Regular/Thin architecture rebuild, same pattern as SD-4/SD-5's 2026-07-17 purge (which also removed 12x4/10x8, not present in RG-2's original 9 to begin with).
_meta.BUGFIX_2026-07-24: 4 of 32 breakpoints were WRONG when first copied from SD-4/SD-5's (also-wrong-at-the-time) data: 12x10/20x6: 432->360, 22x6: 462->385, 12x12/24x6: 528->440, 18x10/30x6: 666->555. Root cause and fix identical to the SD-4/SD-5 fix documented in that family's own lookup_data JSON -- see SD-4_SD-5_272RL_sidewall.json for full detail. Both families were fixed the same day, this JSON reflects the corrected/re-sorted values.

breakpoints_ascending_by_max_cfm.note: final/ceiling tier - also the unconditioned else clause in the formula
